task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles the edge case where an empty list is input.
trace:
**

I started by checking if the input list `nums` is empty, in which case I return `None`. If not, I sort the list and determine its length. Depending on whether the length of the list is odd or even, I either return the middle element (for an odd length) or the average of the two middle elements (for an even length). This approach ensures that the function handles both cases correctly and efficiently.
